title: Lock contention in OrderService write path
lede: Investigation into the p99 OrderService write spike that started at 14:02 UTC on 2026-06-15.

summary.question: Why are p99 OrderService writes spiking after 14:00 UTC on 2026-06-15?
summary.context: Investigated traces, code, and recent deploys in the services/order repo on 2026-06-15. Pulled trace bundles for the 14:00 to 15:30 UTC window, read relevant source, and reviewed the last seven days of merged PRs.

findings.text: A new pessimistic lock was introduced in PR #4421 and merged 2026-06-10.
The lock window in Write() encloses a 200ms external call to PricingService.
Traffic doubled at 14:02 UTC due to a flash promo, exposing the contention.
p99 returns to normal when the external call is moved outside the lock (reproduced locally).

confidence.reason: Reproduced locally with a synthetic load matching production traffic shape.

recommendations.text: Move the PricingService call outside the lock; see proposed diff.
Add a perf regression test in CI that fails if Write() p99 > 200ms under 2x load.

openQuestions.text: Should we cap the lock duration with a context timeout? Would shed load earlier but might break the consistency contract.

openQuestions: What does PricingService p99 look like under multi-region failover? Not exercised in today's traffic.
outOfScope: Read-path latency (separate code path, no contention)
PricingService internals (treated as a black box; team owns it)

blocks.text: p99 write latency began rising at 14:02 UTC, correlated with traffic, not a deploy. The most recent deploy of order was at 12:14 UTC and had been quiet for 1h48m before the spike.

blocks.caption: p99 write latency vs RPS, 13:30 to 15:30 UTC

blocks.text: PR #4421 introduced a pessimistic lock around the entire Write() body to fix a separate race. The lock now encloses a synchronous call to pricing.GetQuote(ctx), which has a steady-state p50 of 80ms and p99 of 200ms.

blocks.caption: Lock acquisition site (current state)

blocks.text: Move the external call outside the lock. The lock only needs to guard persist(), which is the operation with the race condition that PR #4421 was originally fixing.

lines: func (s *Service) Write(ctx context.Context, o Order) error {

blocks.text: Re-running the synthetic load with this change brings p99 back to 158ms at the same 2x RPS.

blocks.caption: Synthetic load p99 (5 runs each, ms)

meta.prompt: Investigate why p99 OrderService writes are spiking after 14:00 UTC on 2026-06-15. Write it up as an html report.

tools.name: view
tools.summary: read source files in write path

sources.title: PR #4421 — add pessimistic lock for OrderWrite
